text: SHIELD INSURANCE CO. Travel Insurance Policy Policy Type Domestic & International Travel Insurance Policy Code TRAVEL-STD-2024 Valid For Individual and Family Travel Plans Policy Duration Per trip or Annual Multi-trip Claim Window Within 90 days of incident 1. COVERAGE OVERVIEW This travel insurance policy provides financial protection against unexpected events during domestic and international travel. Coverage includes flight disruptions, baggage loss, and medical emergencies. Claims must be supported by original documentation and filed within 90 days. 2. COVERED CLAIM CATEGORIES 2.1 Flight Delay Coverage is provided when a scheduled flight is delayed by 3 or more hours due to reasons beyond the passenger's control including airline operational issues, air traffic control delays, technical faults, or adverse weather conditions.  Eligible delay: Minimum 3 hours from scheduled departure time.  Required documents: Boarding pass, airline-issued delay certificate or notification.  Booking reference number must be present on submitted documents.  Airline name and flight date must be clearly visible in the document.  Compensation: Rs. 500 per hour of delay up to a maximum of Rs. 5,000.  Delays due to strikes, political unrest, or government actions are also covered.  Voluntary rebooking or passenger-initiated changes are not covered. 2.2 Flight Cancellation Coverage is provided when a confirmed flight is cancelled by the airline and the passenger is unable to reach their destination within 24 hours of original departure.  Eligible cancellation: Airline-initiated cancellation with less than 4 hours notice.  Required documents: Booking confirmation, cancellation notice from airline, boarding pass.  Booking reference and travel date must be present in submitted documents.  Compensation covers non-refundable ticket costs up to Rs. 10,000.  Additional accommodation and meal expenses covered up to Rs. 3,000.  Cancellations due to passenger no-show or voluntary cancellations are excluded.  Claims must include proof that

the airline did not provide an alternative flight. 2.3 Luggage Lost Coverage applies when checked-in baggage is confirmed lost by the airline and not recovered within 24 hours of the passenger arriving at the destination.  Required documents: Baggage claim receipt (booking reference), Property Irregularity Report (PIR) from airline.  Booking reference number must appear on the baggage claim receipt.  Compensation: Up to Rs. 20,000 per bag for lost luggage.  Delayed baggage (not lost) is compensated up to Rs. 5,000 after 12 hours.  High-value items such as electronics and jewellery require prior declaration.  Fragile items, cash, and travel documents are not covered under this clause.  Self-carried or cabin baggage lost due to passenger negligence is excluded. 3. CLAIM ELIGIBILITY CONDITIONS  Policy must be active and cover the dates of travel at time of incident.  Claim must be filed within 90 days of the incident occurring.  Original supporting documents must be submitted — photocopies are not accepted.  Documents must not be digitally edited or tampered with.  The claim category selected must match the type of documents submitted.  Duplicate claims for the same incident will be automatically rejected.  The booking reference number must be visible in all submitted documents.  Travel dates and destination must match the policy coverage period. 4. CLAIM SETTLEMENT PROCESS Claims are processed through our Straight-Through Processing (STP) system:  Step 1: Claimant selects claim category (Flight Delay / Flight Cancellation / Luggage Lost).  Step 2: Claim form is submitted with policy number, travel details, and supporting document photo.  Step 3: System validates policy status and checks for duplicate document submissions.  Step 4: OCR engine extracts key fields from the document (booking reference, airline, date, amount).  Step 5: Consistency check verifies extracted

fields match the selected claim category.  Step 6: Policy coverage is verified against the claim description using AI reasoning.  Step 7: A composite score is computed. Score >= 0.75 triggers automatic approval and settlement.  Step 8: Approved claims are credited within 2-3 business days. 5. SETTLEMENT LIMITS Claim Category Maximum Settlement Flight Delay (per hour, max 10hrs) Rs. 500/hr — Max Rs. 5,000 Flight Cancellation (ticket cost) Rs. 10,000 Flight Cancellation (accommodation + meals) Rs. 3,000 Luggage Lost (per bag) Rs. 20,000 Luggage Delayed (after 12hrs) Rs. 5,000 Total Maximum Per Trip Rs. 50,000 6. REQUIRED DOCUMENTS PER CATEGORY Claim Category Mandatory Documents Key Fields OCR Must Find Flight Delay Boarding pass + Delay certificate Booking ref, Airline name, Date Flight Cancellation Booking confirmation + Cancellation notice Booking ref, Date Luggage Lost Baggage claim receipt + PIR from airline Booking ref 7. EXCLUSIONS  Delays or cancellations caused by the passenger (late check-in, no-show).  Voluntary flight changes or upgrades initiated by the passenger.  Losses due to passenger negligence (leaving bags unattended).  Claims without a valid booking reference number in the documents.  Incidents occurring outside the policy coverage period.  Claims filed after the 90-day window from the date of incident.  Losses already compensated by the airline or another insurance policy. For queries contact: claims@shieldinsurance.com | Helpline: 1800-123-4567
